title: CS Mioveni

infobox.value: (Romanian pronunciation: [[ˌklubul sporˈtiv mi.oˈvenʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")), commonly known as **CS Mioveni** or simply **Mioveni**, was a Romanian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [club](/wiki/Club_(organization) "Club (organization)") based in [Mioveni](/wiki/Mioveni "Mioveni"), [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County").
* Best finish in Liga I: 12th in [2021–22](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Liga_I#League_table "2021–22 Liga I")

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football club in Mioveni, Romania

Not to be confused with [FC Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești").

Football club

CS Mioveni

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [CS Mioveni crest](/wiki/File:Cs_mioveni.png) | |
| Full name | Clubul Sportiv Mioveni |
| Nicknames | *Galben-verzii* (The Yellow and Greens) |
| Short name | Mioveni |
| Founded | 2000 as *AS Mioveni 2000* |
| Dissolved | 2025 |
| Ground | [Orășenesc](/wiki/Stadionul_Or%C4%83%C8%99enesc_(Mioveni) "Stadionul Orășenesc (Mioveni)") |
| Capacity | 10,000[[1]](#cite_note-stadium-1) |
| Website | [csmioveni.ro](https://csmioveni.ro/) |

**Clubul Sportiv Mioveni** (Romanian pronunciation: [[ˌklubul sporˈtiv mi.oˈvenʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")), commonly known as **CS Mioveni** or simply **Mioveni**, was a Romanian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [club](/wiki/Club_(organization) "Club (organization)") based in [Mioveni](/wiki/Mioveni "Mioveni"), [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County").

The team was founded in 2000 as *AS Mioveni* and began playing the [fourth division](/wiki/Liga_IV "Liga IV"). The following year, it merged with nearby [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești") and took its berth in the [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"). The club made its first appearance in the top division in the [2007–08 campaign](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Liga_I "2007–08 Liga I") as *Dacia Mioveni*, and in 2010 settled on the current name of CS Mioveni.

"The Yellow and Greens" played their home matches at the [Stadionul Orășenesc](/wiki/Stadionul_Or%C4%83%C8%99enesc_(Mioveni) "Stadionul Orășenesc (Mioveni)"), which had a seating capacity of 10,000 persons.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

For the history of Dacia Pitești, see [FC Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești").

### First years and ascension (2000–2011)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: First years and ascension (2000–2011)")]

The club was founded in 2000 under the name *AS Mioveni* (Mioveni Sports Association). After one season in the [Liga IV](/wiki/Liga_IV "Liga IV"), AS Mioveni merged with [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "Dacia Pitești") in 2001 and took its place in the [Liga III](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"), while the club changed its name to *AS Dacia Mioveni*, only to change it soon after that to *CS Dacia Mioveni* (Dacia Mioveni Sports Club).

In its first season of division football, Dacia finished 3rd in the [Liga III](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"). The next season however, the team finished top of series IV of the Liga III and therefore, in the summer of 2003 they promoted to the [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II") where they activated for four years without any outstanding performance.

At the end of the 2006–07 season, Dacia Mioveni finished runner-up in the [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"), Seria II, and promoted for the first time in history to the [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I").

Dacia's best performance was the only appearance in the [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"), in the [2007–08](/wiki/Liga_I_2007%E2%80%9308 "Liga I 2007–08") season, when they finished 16th and were relegated. During the same season Dacia Mioveni reached the semi-finals of the [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României"), being eliminated by [CFR Cluj](/wiki/CFR_Cluj "CFR Cluj"), after an impressive win in the quarterfinals against [Dinamo București](/wiki/Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "Dinamo București"), with 1–0.

| Period | Name |
| --- | --- |
| 2000–2001 | *AS Mioveni 2000* |
| 2001–2010 | *Dacia Mioveni* |
| 2010–present | **CS Mioveni** |

In the summer of 2010 the club was renamed, *CS Mioveni* being the new name. The club officials took this decision because [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia") refused to sponsor the club, instead sponsoring Italian club [Udinese Calcio](/wiki/Udinese_Calcio "Udinese Calcio").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Even if the club had finished the [2010-11 Liga II](/wiki/2010-11_Liga_II "2010-11 Liga II") season on the third position, the club promoted in the [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") because the second placed [FC Bihor Oradea](/wiki/FC_Bihor_Oradea_(1958) "FC Bihor Oradea (1958)") had problems with the licence.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### A second league constant and a new promotion (2011–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: A second league constant and a new promotion (2011–present)")]

CS Mioveni relegated again in the [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II") at the end of the [2011–12](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_Liga_I "2011–12 Liga I") edition, after finishing on the bottom of the league, with only 12 points won in 34 rounds. After this season, "the yellow and greens" spent no less than 9 years in the antechamber of the [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") top-flight, the team from [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia")'s town becoming a classic of the [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"). Most of the time, Mioveni was too good to relegate in the third tier, but not good enough to promote back in the first division. In these nine years, the club obtain the following rankings: 2nd ([2014–15](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Liga_II "2014–15 Liga II")), 3rd ([2019–20](/wiki/2019%E2%80%9320_Liga_II "2019–20 Liga II")), 4th ([2015–16](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Liga_II "2015–16 Liga II"), [2016–17](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_Liga_II "2016–17 Liga II")), 7th ([2018–19](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_Liga_II "2018–19 Liga II")), 8th ([2012–13](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Liga_II "2012–13 Liga II"), [2013–14](/wiki/2013%E2%80%9314_Liga_II "2013–14 Liga II")) and 9th ([2017–18](/wiki/2017%E2%80%9318_Liga_II "2017–18 Liga II")).

Mioveni promoted back to the [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") at the end of the [2020–21](/wiki/2020%E2%80%9321_Liga_II "2020–21 Liga II") season, when after a ranking on the 3rd place, they won the promotion/relegation play-offs (2–1 on aggregate) against top-flight club [FC Hermannstadt](/wiki/FC_Hermannstadt "FC Hermannstadt").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Ground

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ground")]

Main article: [Stadionul Orășenesc (Mioveni)](/wiki/Stadionul_Or%C4%83%C8%99enesc_(Mioveni) "Stadionul Orășenesc (Mioveni)")

CS Mioveni plays its home games on [Stadionul Orășenesc](/wiki/Stadionul_Or%C4%83%C8%99enesc_(Mioveni) "Stadionul Orășenesc (Mioveni)"), a 10,000-seat arena, in downtown Mioveni. Between 2013 and 2015 the stadium was renovated and "the yellow and greens" played their home matches on [Nicolae Dobrin Stadium](/wiki/Stadionul_Nicolae_Dobrin "Stadionul Nicolae Dobrin") in [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"). Second team of the club, CS Mioveni II, also used to play its home matches on Colibași Stadium, stadium used also by the first team as a training ground.

## Support

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Support")]

CS Mioveni has never had many supporters in [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"), most of the public opting for much more familiar and successful [FC Argeș](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești"). Over the time the club had sporadically an organized group of supporters, especially between 2006 and 2011, when the club was in the [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"), twice and important rivalries with FC Argeș were born.

### Rivalries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Rivalries")]

CS Mioveni does not have many important rivalries, the only important one is against [FC Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești"), commonly known as *Argeș Derby* or the *Derby of [Argeș](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County")*. In the past, Mioveni had also a local rivalry against [Internațional Curtea de Argeș](/wiki/FC_Interna%C8%9Bional_Curtea_de_Arge%C8%99 "FC Internațional Curtea de Argeș").

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Honours")]

### Domestic

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Domestic")]

#### Leagues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Leagues")]

* **[Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II")**
  + Runners-up (2): [2006–07](/wiki/2006%E2%80%9307_Liga_II "2006–07 Liga II"), [2014–15](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Liga_II "2014–15 Liga II")
  + Play-off Winners (1): [2020–21](/wiki/2020-21_Liga_II "2020-21 Liga II")
* **[Liga III](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III")**
  + **Winners (1):** [2002–03](/wiki/2002%E2%80%9303_Divizia_C "2002–03 Divizia C")

#### Other performances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CS_Mioveni&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Other performances")]

* Appearances in [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): **4**
* Best finish in Liga I: 12th in [2021–22](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Liga_I#League_table "2021–22 Liga I")
* Place 75 of 101 teams in [Liga I All-time table](/wiki/Liga_I#All-time_table "Liga I")
* Semi-finalist of [2007–08 Cupa României](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "2007–08 Cupa României")
